result:
**I'd make curiosity the default human operating system.**

Not forced kindness, not mandatory peace, not even unlimited energy. Just this: every single person wakes up with an *alive*, almost uncomfortable need to understand how things actually work. The kind of curiosity that makes dogma feel like bad air—something you instinctively reject.

If that one switch flipped, most of our worst problems would become temporary engineering challenges instead of eternal tribal battlegrounds. We'd still argue. We'd still have egos. But the *direction* of the species would tilt toward reality instead of comforting fiction.

Everything else—cooperation, innovation, even wisdom—tends to follow real curiosity like a shadow. The rest is just noise.
